last_assistant_message:
The Shrooms directory isn't a git repo — it's just the working directory holding everything. The SPEC is local at `/Users/jh/Development/Shrooms/SPEC.md`. You can reference it when you start building the frontend.

All pushed:
- **api-shrooms** → `Johannes0501/api-shrooms` (Dockerfile fix + lockfile)
- **openshrooly** → `Johannes0501/openshrooly` (WiFi config + API posting + build flags)

Already done — it was forked earlier and I just pushed your changes to `Johannes0501/openshrooly`. Your fork is at https://github.com/Johannes0501/openshrooly with all the modifications (WiFi, API posting, build flags).

You're all set to hack on it freely.

**Current repo:** `Johannes0501/shrooms` — the ShroomHub Next.js dashboard (this working directory)

**Embedded repos (separate):**
- `Johannes0501/api-shrooms` — the API backend
- `Johannes0501/openshrooly` — the ESP32/ESPHome device firmware

As for the **agentic template** — I don't see a dedicated template repo in these remotes. The `.agentic/` directory structure (expertise, memory, specs) and `.claude/` directory (skills, agents, commands) exist here in `shrooms`. Do you mean:

1. A separate template repo on your GitHub (e.g. `Johannes0501/agentic-template` or similar)?
2. The `.agentic/` + `.claude/` scaffolding within this repo that you reuse across projects?

Could you clarify what you're referring to so I can find it?

Makes sense — you want to `git clone` one repo on another PC and have all three projects ready to go.

**Git submodules** is the cleanest way to do this. The `shrooms` repo becomes your workspace, and `api-shrooms` + `openshrooly` are tracked as submodules. On another PC you'd just:

```
git clone --recursive https://github.com/Johannes0501/shrooms.git
```

Want me to:
1. Add `api-shrooms/` and `openshrooly/` as git submodules (pointing to their GitHub repos)
2. Update `.gitignore` accordingly
3. Commit the log changes, `.claude/data/`, and submodule references

All three repos stay independent — you can commit/push each separately — but the parent repo tracks which commit of each to use.

Want me to go ahead?

The safety hook blocked `rm -rf`. Could you run this yourself? Type:

```
! rm -rf api-shrooms openshrooly
```

This removes the local copies so we can re-add them as submodules. (The repos are safe on GitHub — nothing is lost.)
